The only thing I've changed here is the purchase, sorry, is the pixel optimized event. So view content, ATC, etc, etc. And it has a direct correlation to the CPM. So what does that mean? What that means is Facebook looks at what the value is to you. If you want to go for the view content event, which is further up the funnel, there's more people that Facebook will give you access to and actually more people that view content on websites that don't necessarily purchase. The available auctions there is far higher. So therefore, the CPM is cheaper. So you're not necessarily gonna be charged the same. Now, this is why I do graduation testing with cheaper CPMs because I can get more traffic through, more clicks. And I'm taking a trade off between my premium audience. but then you can see the traffic come through. I've had 11 clicks from purchase into my product page. And here I've had 37.
